sections.paragraph: Jos 15:50, and אְשְׁתְּמֹועַ (“obedience,” as if infinit. Conj. VIII, from the root שָׁמַע), [Eshtemoh, Eshtemoa], Jos 21:14 1Sa 30:28 1Ch 4:17, 19 1Ch 4:19 6:42 , pr.n. of a Levitical city in the mountains of Judah. [Now prob. called Semûa, or es-Semûa, السموع; “a considerable village situated on a low hill.” Rob. ii. 626.]
